measurement: SG per-range-band PELLET-LANDING FRACTION (how many of the 10 pellets land on the boss body per shot), via damage arithmetic (popup value + colour classification), NOT raw popup counts alone
purpose: Test the engine's SG_OUT_OF_NEAR_HIT_FRACTION (sim.ts:157, CALIBRATED FLAG): near = all 10 pellets credited (1.0), everywhere else = 0.3 (3 pellets). A new footage read suggested ~5-8 pellet popups/shot, contradicting 1.0 at near; this measures the TRUE per-band landing fraction and separates it from render-dropout.

measuredValues.note: Near windows coincide with self-burst Full Burst (white 33374 vs 25672 elsewhere). Landing fraction is a geometry/hit-rate quantity and is FB-INDEPENDENT, so the elevated near values do not bias the landing measurement.

part1_damageArithmeticValidation.question: Does one rendered popup = one landed pellet, or do popups under-report landed pellets (invisible-X render dropout / pellet-value-merging)?
part1_damageArithmeticValidation.method: On readable NEAR clusters (num_023 v~46.9, near1_v53.35, near2_v160.5), read every popup's VALUE and COLOUR, then classify: white=1x pellet, orange/crit=1x pellet (value ~1.4-1.5x), red CORE=1x pellet (value 2x). The distinguishing test for MERGE-dropout: does any WHITE popup carry 2x the white base (66748) or 3x, which would mean multiple pellets summed into one number? A 2x-value RED popup is a core (1 pellet); a 2x-value WHITE popup would be 2 merged pellets.
observations: Every readable WHITE popup carries the 1x base value (33374 at near-FB; 25672 elsewhere). No white popup at 66748 (2x) or higher was observed. Overlapping clusters are multiple DISTINCT 33374 numbers stacked, not a single summed 66748.
Orange crit popups read 46210 (1 pellet). Red 'CORE HIT' popups read 51345/71882/559046 (1 pellet each, weak-point core).
coreband2 R2 already CONFIRMED: off-boss pellets render NOTHING; on-boss pellets each render exactly one popup at the crosshair.
part1_damageArithmeticValidation.impliedVsCount: Because every popup carries a single-pellet value (1x white / 1x crit / 1x core) and never a merged 2x-white, IMPLIED landed pellets (sum_damage / white_base, with crit/core weighted to 1 pellet each) equals the popup COUNT to within measurement noise. implied/count ~= 1.0.
part1_damageArithmeticValidation.interpretation: Popups do NOT merge pellets, so the per-shot popup COUNT is a faithful count of pellets that LAND ON THE BOSS BODY and render. 6 popups means ~6 landed pellets, NOT ~8-10. The only residual dropout channel (an on-body pellet that renders no popup at all) is bounded small by the global damage cross-check (part2b) and by the fact that the gappy spider-mech silhouette physically explains the ~4 missing pellets/shot as pellets flying through open gaps (visible sky between the mech's limbs in every frame).
part1_damageArithmeticValidation.confidence: medium-high for 'no value-merging' (directly observed); medium for the absolute claim that no invisible on-body dropout exists (bounded, not proven to zero).

part2_perBandLandingFraction.method: landed pellets/shot = pooled pellet popups / shots sampled (coreband2 windows), x dropout factor (1.0). Fraction = landed/10. Wilson 95% CI computed on shots*10 pellet-trials; a further +/-0.10-0.15 SYSTEMATIC band is added for popup overlap/bloom counting error (coreband2's stated ~+/-30% on pooled pellet totals).

near.bossDistanceNote: close/large in readable windows, BUT distance varies widely inside the near band (seen large AND receded within v40-77 / v151-183) -> extra spread; coincides with self-burst FB.

mid.bossDistanceNote: medium (center). Numbers legible against grey sky -> most reliable band.

far.bossDistanceNote: distant/small + smoke + frequent reloading -> fewer readable shots (28<40).

part2_perBandLandingFraction.shape: Landing is FLAT-ish across bands (0.45-0.60), NOT a near=1.0 / else=0.3 step. Near and mid are essentially equal (~0.60); far is the lowest (~0.45); midfar intermediate (~0.55). Distance correlates weakly with landing (far < midfar ~ near ~ mid).

part2b_globalDamageCrossCheck.logic: The popup COUNT gives the relative per-band SHAPE; the 53.97M total anchors the absolute LEVEL. If the popup-profile landing (dropout=1) reconstructs ~the full total (after subtracting Burst III casts + skill2), there is no shortfall requiring a hidden-pellet population -> dropout ~= 1. A large invisible-X population would show up as SG damage far below (total - burst - skill2).
part2b_globalDamageCrossCheck.roughReconstruction: Using measured pellet values (25672 non-FB, 33374 FB), the popup-profile landing (~6/6/4.5/5.5 per band) over a plausible ~250-360 SG shots produces SG damage of the same order as (total - burst - skill2). Reconstruction is LOOSE because the self-burst cast count is not pinned (burst 1254%, ~2-5.9M/cast) and shot count is estimated, so it CANNOT precisely fix the dropout factor. It does establish that popup-profile landing already accounts for roughly the whole total -- there is no room for a large (>=1.5x) invisible-pellet inflation, and dropout>=1 is required physically (each popup is a real hit).
part2b_globalDamageCrossCheck.conclusion: Consistent with dropout ~= 1.0; excludes dropout large enough to push near back to ~1.0. Not precise enough to resolve a modest (<=1.3x) invisible-X component.

engineComparison.engineConstant: SG_OUT_OF_NEAR_HIT_FRACTION = 0.3 (sim.ts:157, CALIBRATED FLAG); near band credited 1.0 (all 10 pellets), all other bands 0.3 (3 pellets).

near.verdict: engine OVER-credits by ~1.7x -- near is NOT all-10. ~4 pellets/shot miss through the gappy spider-mech silhouette even up close.

engineComparison.predictedDirection: The brief predicted landing is HIGHER than the flat 0.3 at mid/far (engine runs SG cold at range). CONFIRMED for mid/far/midfar (0.45-0.60 vs 0.30). ADDITIONALLY, the opposite-direction surprise also holds: near is well BELOW 1.0 (~0.60), so the engine runs SG HOT at near on this boss. Net: true landing is much flatter (~0.45-0.60 across all bands) than the engine's 1.0->0.3 step; BOTH edges of the engine model are wrong on this boss.

keyAnswers.isNearReally1.0: NO. Near landing ~= 0.60 (about 6 of 10 pellets land), not 1.0. The gappy multi-part boss lets ~4 pellets/shot pass through gaps even at close range.
keyAnswers.isMidFarReally0.3: NO. Mid 0.60, far 0.45, midfar 0.55 -- all well above 0.3.
keyAnswers.dropoutFactor: ~1.0 (6 popups means ~6 pellets, not ~8). White popups carry single-pellet values with no 2x merges; off-boss pellets render nothing; the global total leaves no room for a large hidden-pellet population.
keyAnswers.contradictsEngineInPredictedDirection: YES for mid/far/midfar (measured > 0.3, engine under-credits SG at range). ALSO contradicts at near in the opposite direction (measured 0.60 < engine 1.0).

caveats: Popup counts carry ~+/-30% (overlap + red spider-lily hit-VFX blooms with no numbers + red-on-dark visibility) per coreband2 -> landing fractions carry ~+/-0.10-0.15 systematic on top of the Wilson CI.
Near boss DISTANCE varies widely inside the near band (observed large and receded within the same window) -> near landing has extra real spread; the 0.60 is a window-weighted average.
Red-on-dark visibility understates cores/values at near (mild lower bound); far numbers on grey sky are reliable.

Residual invisible-X (an on-body pellet rendering no popup) cannot be excluded to zero; if present it is modest (global-total-bounded). Measured fractions are therefore best read as LOWER BOUNDS on true landing, with the total capping how much higher near could be (roughly <=0.7-0.8, not 1.0).

SINGLE boss (multi-part spider mech with a gappy silhouette and a small central weak-point gem). Do NOT over-generalize the ABSOLUTE fractions across bosses/weapons. The robust, transferable finding is QUALITATIVE: near < 1.0, range > 0.3, landing much flatter than the engine's 1.0/0.3 step.
Self-burst FB elevates near pellet values (33374 vs 25672) but not landing (geometry); confirmed FB-independent.
confidence: Overall medium. The qualitative result (near ~0.6 not 1.0; mid/far/midfar ~0.45-0.60 not 0.3; landing flat not stepped; popups = landed pellets with no merge dropout) is robust and is supported by two independent reads plus the value-arithmetic and global-total checks. Absolute fractions carry ~+/-0.10-0.15. This is a single boss.
